purpose: Stress-test #3. Animals chosen as the richest test of the tuned rules: genuinely orthogonal, OBJECTIVELY-measurable axes (mph, PSI, LD50, dB, years, grams), dense reflex-defeaters, and a clean listable answer set (famous species) that forces answer-kind consistency. Tests whether the skill keeps the set off the obvious size/danger clusters. Every contested superlative web-verified. NOT filed to the library.

cluster_audit.verdict: 12 questions, largest cluster = 2/12 (17%, danger). All answers are species (answer-kind consistent). Every dimension anchored to an objective measure.

candidates.q: Which animal moves faster than any other — reaching its top speed in a steep dive?
candidates.note: ~240 mph in a stoop. Head fake: the cheetah (~70 mph) is only the fastest on land; the 'in a dive' clause is the tell. Finalists peregrine falcon, cheetah, sailfish, golden eagle.

candidates.q: Which animal is responsible for more human deaths each year than any other?
candidates.note: ~600,000-1,000,000/yr via malaria, dengue, etc. Classic head fake: the mind reaches for sharks, snakes, big cats. Finalists mosquito, snake, dog (rabies), human.

candidates.q: Which living animal can clamp its jaws shut with more force than any other?
candidates.note: ~3,700 PSI measured (Erickson 2012) vs hippo ~1,800, great white less. Finalists saltwater crocodile, hippo, great white shark, jaguar.

candidates.q: Drop for drop, which snake carries the most toxic venom of all?
candidates.note: Lowest LD50 of any snake. Reveal nuance (and head fake vs #2): it's shy and almost never kills people — most toxic is not most deadly. Finalists inland taipan, black mamba, king cobra, Russell's viper.

candidates.q: Which backboned animal lives longer than any other?
candidates.note: ~272-400 yrs (radiocarbon eye-lens dating); next is the bowhead whale at ~211. Metric pinned to 'backboned' since some clams/jellyfish go further. Finalists Greenland shark, bowhead whale, giant tortoise, koi.

candidates.q: Which mammal weighs less than any other?
candidates.note: Lighter than a penny. Flag: the bumblebee bat is the smallest by body length/skull — metric pinned to weight. Finalists Etruscan shrew, bumblebee bat, pygmy mouse.

candidates.q: Which animal makes a louder sound than any other on Earth?
candidates.note: Echolocation clicks ~230 dB underwater; pistol shrimp ~189, blue whale ~188. Defeats the lion/howler-monkey reflex. Finalists sperm whale, pistol shrimp, blue whale, howler monkey.

candidates.q: Which animal has more legs than any other?
candidates.note: 1,306 legs — the first to truly out-leg its name (mille = thousand). Finalists millipede, centipede, caterpillar.

candidates.q: Which animal travels the farthest in its yearly migration?
candidates.note: Pole to pole, ~70,000-90,000 km a year — it sees two summers and more daylight than any animal. Finalists Arctic tern, sooty shearwater, humpback whale, monarch butterfly.

candidates.q: Which backboned animal can release more eggs at once than any other?
candidates.note: Up to ~300 million eggs per spawning — the most fecund vertebrate. Finalists ocean sunfish, cod, sturgeon, sea turtle.

candidates.q: Which bird has been recorded flying higher than any other?
candidates.note: ~37,000 ft — one struck an airliner there in 1973. Defeats the eagle/goose reflex (bar-headed goose is the high-altitude migrant, but lower). Finalists Rüppell's vulture, bar-headed goose, bald eagle, whooper swan.

candidates.q: Which bird outnumbers every other bird on the planet?
candidates.note: ~33 billion alive at any time. Flag/reveal: the red-billed quelea (~1.5 billion) is the most numerous WILD bird — a fair near-miss the reveal should credit. Finalists domestic chicken, red-billed quelea, house sparrow, pigeon.
